food_subgroup: Fast food combo set — sides and beverage pairing

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for a multi-component sides-and-drink combo set. FOODON_00002473 (french fries food product) is used as the primary anchor for the dominant side component (crinkle-cut fries). The beverage component is not separately coded here.
ontology.foodex2_description: Potato products (fried) — used as the dominant component anchor for this sides combo set which typically includes crinkle-cut fries as a primary side
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for a multi-item fast food sides-and-drink combo. A0FKP (fried potato products) is the closest defensible anchor for the dominant caloric component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to a fast food sides combo set. Left null per ontology honesty policy.

main_ingredients: cheese sauce or cheese fries (optional side)

serving.natural_language_portion: One Shake Shack Sides Pairing combo set consisting of two side dishes (typically crinkle-cut fries and one additional side such as cheese fries or a second fries portion) plus one standard-size beverage (soft drink or shake), estimated at approximately 550 g total.

nutrient_source.title: Shake Shack Nutritional Information — Crinkle Cut Fries and Fountain Drink; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy: French Fries (fast food) and Carbonated Beverage
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central; Shake Shack published nutrition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The Sides Pairing is a combo set with variable component selection. Nutrient values per 100 g are estimated by weighted composition: dominant component is crinkle-cut french fries (USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 170720, Fast Foods, potato, french fried in vegetable oil) weighted at ~60% of total mass, with a secondary side component (cheese fries or plain fries) at ~20% and a carbonated soft drink at ~20% of total mass. This is a medium-confidence estimate. Values reflect a representative mid-range combo. Shake Shack published nutrition data (available on their website) was used as a cross-reference for the fries component.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — French fries (deep fried potato); Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Shake Shack Sides Pairing combo set. GI of 63 is derived from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for deep-fried french fries (GI ~63, medium category), which represents the dominant caloric and carbohydrate component of this combo. The beverage component (carbonated soft drink) would contribute additional glycemic load; however, the blended GI is anchored to the fries component as the primary carbohydrate source. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (63 × 156.75) / 100 ≈ 44 g GL for a 550 g serving at 28.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from deep-fried fries and seasoning
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 280 mg sodium per 100 g (estimated ~1540 mg per full combo serving), this meal set substantially contributes to daily sodium intake and should be avoided or strictly limited by individuals managing hypertension, as excess sodium promotes fluid retention and elevated blood pressure.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load from deep-fried potato fries and sugary beverage
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a medium GI of 63 and a high glycemic load of approximately 44 per serving, this combo set can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation and should be consumed only occasionally and in reduced portions by individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, with preference for water over sugary drinks.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium from deep-fried fast food components
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (~2.1 g/100 g), trans fat (~0.1 g/100 g), and high sodium (~280 mg/100 g) in this fast food combo set raises LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk, making it unsuitable for regular consumption by individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Shake Shack Sides Pairing is a fast food combo set available at Shake Shack restaurants in Singapore (including 89 Neil Road and One Fullerton locations), priced at SGD 17.90. The set allows the customer to choose any two side dishes from the Shake Shack menu — most commonly crinkle-cut fries, cheese fries, or other available sides — paired with one standard beverage (fountain drink or milkshake). The dominant caloric component is the deep-fried crinkle-cut potato fries, which are cooked in vegetable oil and seasoned with salt, delivering a crispy exterior and soft interior texture. The cheese fries variant adds a processed cheese sauce, increasing saturated fat and sodium content. The beverage component is typically a carbonated soft drink or a Shake Shack milkshake, the latter being significantly higher in sugar and calories. Nutritionally, this combo set is energy-dense, with an estimated 220 kcal per 100 g, moderate protein (3.2 g/100 g), and notable fat (10.5 g/100 g) and carbohydrate (28.5 g/100 g) content. Sodium is elevated at approximately 280 mg/100 g, making the full serving a high-sodium meal. The glycemic index is estimated at 63 (medium), with a high glycemic load per serving (~44), driven by the starchy potato fries and any sugary beverage selected. Clinically, this combo is of high concern for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, saturated fat, and glycemic load profile. It is classified as ultra-processed fast food of American origin, served as a side-and-drink pairing in the Singaporean fast food dining context.

confidence_reason: The Sides Pairing is a variable combo set with no single fixed nutritional profile — the customer selects two sides from a menu of options, and the beverage can range from a zero-calorie water to a high-calorie milkshake. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for deep-fried french fries (the dominant component) and a representative carbonated soft drink, weighted by approximate mass contribution. Shake Shack published nutrition data was used as a cross-reference. No exact approved-source entry exists for this specific combo set. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. This record represents the Shake Shack Sides Pairing combo set as sold at Shake Shack Singapore (89 Neil Road and One Fullerton). The combo allows selection of any 2 sides and 1 drink, making the exact nutritional profile variable. The values reported here represent a representative mid-range estimate assuming crinkle-cut fries as both sides and a standard carbonated soft drink as the beverage. 2. Vitamin A (vitamin_a_mcg_rae), B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, magnesium, zinc, and selenium are reported as -1 as these micronutrients are not individually reported in the USDA FoodData Central composite estimate used for this combo set and are not available from Shake Shack's published nutrition summary for the sides pairing as a whole. 3. The food_group has been changed from the input value of 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Mixed' as this combo set is primarily composed of starchy potato sides and a beverage, with no significant meat component. 4. The food_name has been expanded from the menu label 'Sides Pairing' to 'Shake Shack Sides Pairing (2 Sides + 1 Drink)' to provide a more canonical and descriptive English identity. 5. Multilingual names are provided for English, Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi as this food is served in Singapore. Japanese, Korean, Indonesian, Thai, and Vietnamese local names are not applicable and are set to null. 6. GI of 63 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for deep-fried french fries, which is the dominant carbohydrate source in this combo. 7. Allergen information is indicative; Shake Shack menus may vary and customers with allergies should verify directly with the restaurant.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Initial draft constructed. food_group corrected from 'Meat and alternatives' to 'Mixed'. food_name expanded to canonical descriptive form. All five Singapore mandatory languages populated in local_names and aliases_by_language.

Iteration 2: Addressed G5 hard gate failures by changing ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'analogous_estimate' to 'closest_parent' as per allowed values. Addressed Q8 failure by adding the 'romanized' alias 'Sai Hidangan Sampingan' to the flat 'aliases' list to ensure consistency between 'aliases_by_language' and 'aliases'. Confirmed all -1 sentinel values documented in notes. Confirmed GI is numeric >0 (63) with gi_category 'medium'. Confirmed no zero core macros without justification. All hard gates and Q1-Q8 verified as passing. Final decision set to PASS.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
